exemplar.title: How can the design of cone-shaped waffles and cylindrical cups be optimized to minimize material waste while maintaining a constant volume?

detailedExemplarData.title: How can the design of cone-shaped waffles and cylindrical cups be optimized to minimize material waste while maintaining a constant volume?

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear, concise title and research question that focus the exploration on optimisation of cone and cylinder designs
Logical progression of introduction â methodology â calculation â conclusion ensures coherence
Effective use of headings, numbered subsections, tables and figures to structure content
Table 4 succinctly summarizes optimal dimensions, making key results immediately accessible
weaknesses: Table of contents contains redundant overbrace annotations, blank rows and typos that distract from structure
Two sections are both labeled âCalculation 7â, disrupting numeric sequence
Misplaced images and empty table placeholders interrupt the flow
Bibliography entries vary in formatting and some lack complete information
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although some unnecessary repetition and minor organizational flaws (blank rows, duplicate labels, formatting inconsistencies in the ToC and bibliography) prevent an âexceptionally well organisedâ rating, the report is nonetheless highly coherent, logically developed and concise, warranting the top band for Presentation.

strengths: Consistent and accurate use of mathematical notation (Ï, r, h, l, V, A) throughout
Effective structure of numbered methodological steps guiding the reader through the optimisation process
Clear symbol table (Table 1) and wellâintegrated diagrams and GeoGebra graphs
Concise description of second-derivative criteria aids clarity
weaknesses: Occasional formatting slips (missing parentheses, misâtyped squareâroot symbols)
Lengthy derivative expressions lack brief commentary on intermediate steps
Minor mis-numbering of calculations slightly interrupts logical flow
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical Communication is mostly flawless: symbols and notation are used consistently, multiple representations (tables, diagrams, algebra, software graphs) support understanding, and the logical argument is clear. Only minor formatting and numbering issues keep it from perfection, placing it squarely in the top band.

strengths: Genuine personal motivation drawn from involvement in the family business and environmental concerns
Primary data collection and decision to minimise material waste demonstrate independent thinking
Use of digital tools (GeoGebra) and self-collected measurements shows initiative
weaknesses: Exploration relies on a standard optimisation template, limiting the depth of personal innovation
Creative aspects are moderate rather than exceptional
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: There is clear evidence of independent thinking and a personal approach, underpinned by genuine curiosity and initiative. However, the methods follow conventional optimisation processes, placing Personal Engagement solidly in the middle band (2/3).

strengths: Assumptions are well articulated and revisited, showing thoughtful awareness of model limitations
Results are tested with software and the secondâderivative test, indicating a thoughtful analysis
Evaluation includes consideration of how assumptions affect outcomes and suggests a clear extension to cost optimisation
weaknesses: Reflection is concentrated in the introduction and conclusion rather than woven throughout
Analysis of graphical behavior is limited; deeper insights into curvature or trend interpretation are missing
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student offers meaningful reflection on assumptions, model limitations and potential extensions, and provides thoughtful analysis of results and process. However, reflection is not sustained throughout and lacks the depth and multiple perspectives characteristic of the highest band.

strengths: Relevant single-variable calculus is applied effectively to both cone and cylinder optimisation
Mathematical methods are fully commensurate with SL AA expectations, with appropriate use of derivative tests and surfaceâarea functions
GeoGebra graphs rigorously verify second-derivative tests and critical points
Numerical substitutions are clear and reinforce the link between theory and real data
weaknesses: Occasional algebraic slips and unexplained simplifications reduce transparency
Reliance on software for key derivations without brief explanatory commentary
Missing units in tables and symbol definitions limit dimensional clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics is applied at a high SL standard: relevant calculus and algebraic manipulations are used effectively, and technology is integrated to validate results. Although minor errors, missing units and some reliance on software prevent the topmost band, the work demonstrates a thorough understanding and mostly correct execution, meriting a band-5 rating (5/6).

strandSelections.reasoning: Arguments are set out in a logical sequence, derivatives are labelled, and conclusions are clearly linked to earlier results.  Minor discontinuities (e.g. mis-numbered calculations, repeated heading âCalculation 7â) keep it from the âexcellentâ level.

The student demonstrates good conceptual understanding of optimisation and correctly applies the second-derivative test.  However, some algebraic slips (e.g., unexplained simplifications, reliance on software for key steps) and unverified numerical rounding show âminor errorsâ, so band 3 (âgood understandingâ) is most fitting.

The student uses tables of measurements, diagrams of both solids, algebraic working and GeoGebra graphs.  These representations are appropriate and support understanding, satisfying the âeffective useâ descriptor, though the range is not rich enough for the top band.

The report follows a clear sequence (introduction â method â calculation â conclusion).  Each section leads logically to the next and the optimisation argument is easy to follow.  Nevertheless, long digressions (e.g. the extremely long, colour-coded table-of-contents row) and some duplicated explanations break the flow, preventing the âexcellentâ descriptor.

Using digital tools to handle difficult derivatives and incorporating self-collected data shows creativity and initiative above a routine treatment, yet does not reach the âexceptionalâ descriptor.

Results are checked with a second-derivative test and software, and limitations are noted; this constitutes thoughtful analysis but lacks the depth or multiple perspectives required for the highest band.
The student evaluates how assumptions might affect outcomes and suggests an extension to consumer cost optimisation.  This is a clear, though not comprehensive, evaluationâappropriate for band 2.

Assumptions are listed and later revisited; the student reflects on the link between mathematics and sustainability.  Reflection appears mainly in introduction and conclusion rather than âthroughoutâ, fitting the âmeaningfulâ band.

Correct symbols (Ï,r,h,l,V,A) and calculus notation are employed consistently.  A few formatting slips (e.g. missing parentheses, mis-typed square roots) prevent completely flawless communication, placing it in the âmostly consistentâ band.

The exploration is framed around the studentâs family business and environmental concerns, demonstrating a genuine personal approach.  Innovation is moderateâmethods are conventionalâso band 2 is appropriate.

Most content is linked to the optimisation goal, yet the introduction, ToC and several explanations are unnecessarily repetitive, and step-by-step algebra is copied verbatim from software without pruning.  This meets âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elementsâ rather than the higher descriptors.

Optimisation, derivative tests, and surface-area functions are all solidly relevant to the task and are applied to both solids.  The work goes beyond basic geometry but is not wide-ranging enough for âcomprehensiveâ (band 5) or âexpertâ (band 6).

Choosing a personally relevant context, collecting primary measurements and deciding to minimise waste show clear independent thinking beyond textbook examples, but the mathematics itself follows a standard optimisation template, so evidence is âsignificantâ rather than âoutstandingâ.

Headings, numbered subsections, tables and figures give the work an identifiable structure and the reader can locate information easily.  Minor organisational issues (blank table rows, misplaced images) stop it reaching the âexceptionally well organisedâ band.
The mathematics (single-variable calculus, algebraic manipulation, use of technology) is fully within and appropriate to SL AA expectations; there is no material below level, thus band 4 is merited.

annotations.comment: The title and research question are concise and clearly define the explorationâs focus on optimizing cone and cylinder designs.
The introductory statement is split across two lines; consider combining into one succinct sentence to enhance readability.
Table of contents suffers from repeated overbrace annotations and typos in 'introduction', which distract from the structure.
Remove the duplicate overbraces and redundant color-coding in the ToC; simplify entries to a single clear line per section.
Several blank table rows and misplaced symbols in the ToC reduce coherence; ensure all rows convey meaningful content.
The rationale effectively connects personal experience in the family business to environmental concerns, demonstrating genuine curiosity.
A stray math symbol â\mathfrak{S}â appears at the end of the introduction without context; remove or explain its relevance.
The numbered methodology steps are well structured, guiding the reader through each phase of the optimization process.
Step 2 in the methodology is cut mid-sentence (â...in order to express theâ); complete the explanation to avoid ambiguity.
The concise description of the second-derivative criteria ($$f''(x)>0$$, etc.) is accurate and aids mathematical communication.
Add units (cm) to the column headers in Table 4 to reinforce the dimensional consistency of results.
GeoGebra graphs validating the second derivative test demonstrate rigorous verification of critical points.
Two sections are both labeled â[Calculation 7]â, which disrupts the numbering sequence; renumber consistently.
Format Graph 2 in a figure environment and use a caption style consistent with Graph 1 for better presentation.
Incorporate a concrete example by evaluating $$f''$$ numerically for one function to illustrate the test in practice.
Table 1 clearly defines symbols and supports the mathematical derivations effectively.
Include units (e.g., cm) next to variables r and h in the symbol table to reinforce dimensional clarity.
Figures 1 and 2 enrich the report with real-world context and effectively support understanding.
Annotate axes or add dimension labels directly on the figures to clarify which measures correspond to r and h.
Assumptions are well articulated, demonstrating thoughtful awareness of model limitations and simplifying choices.
An empty Table 2 placeholder precedes Table 3, causing a break in flow; consolidate into one table.
Combine Table 2 and Table 3 into a single coherent table and remove the placeholder to improve organization.
Calculation 1 substitutes numerical values clearly, showing the link between formula and real measurements.
The lengthy derivative expressions lack commentary on intermediate steps, reducing clarity for the reader.
When multiplying by $$400\pi^2r^6$$, briefly explain the algebraic goal (e.g., clearing denominators) to guide the reader.
Table 4 header row has multiple empty columns, which distracts from the presentation of optimal dimensions.
Table 4 effectively summarizes the optimal radii and heights, making results immediately accessible.
The discussion of Graph 2 focuses on intersection values; consider analyzing curvature or behavior trends to deepen interpretation.
The conclusion restates key findings clearly and reconnects to personal context, reinforcing the explorationâs relevance.
Streamline the conclusion by removing repeated methodological details; focus on implications and future work.
Bibliography entries vary in formatting and some lack dates; ensure consistency across all citations.
Adopt a consistent citation style and verify that each entry includes author, date, title, and URL.
The Merriam-Webster entry in the bibliography shows a line break and repeated URL, likely an OCR artifact; clean this for clarity.
The bibliography draws on both mathematical and environmental sources, illustrating strong research breadth.
